title: feat: rewrite model registry descriptions for end users
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Rewrites all 132 model descriptions across `shared/registry/` (text, image, video, audio, embeddings, 3D, realtime)
- Removes model title/name duplication from every description — the display name is rendered separately, so `"GPT-5.4 Nano - Fast & Balanced"` becomes `"Fast, affordable all-rounder for everyday chat and image questions"`
- Removes brand/provider attribution from description copy (`brand`/`provider` fields carry that)
- Reframes descriptions for end users: what each model does, what it's good at, and trade-offs (speed vs depth, cost vs quality) so apps can show the list directly to their users
- Description-only change: titles, aliases, pricing, modalities, and `addedDate` untouched; verified by script that no description contains its title or a brand name

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KocSzpGECCMHq8XovmUZbK

gist.headline: Models learned plain English
gist.blurb: All 132 model cards got a small translation layer: less repeated branding, more useful advice. The model picker now says what things do instead of introducing them twice.
gist.summary: Rewrote descriptions for all 132 text, image, video, audio, embedding, 3D, and realtime models to explain user-facing strengths and trade-offs without repeating model names or provider brands.
gist.impact: Apps can now show clearer model lists directly to users, with practical guidance on capabilities, speed, cost, and quality while titles, aliases, pricing, modalities, and added dates remain unchanged.
gist.visual_concept: Depict a cozy model garden where labeled seed packets have grown into clear, helpful little care cards describing what each plant is good at. Polly, wearing tiny librarian glasses, is editing a wall of model cards beside a retro terminal, crossing out duplicated headings and replacing them with friendly capability notes; code-plants and flowers represent the full registry.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot wearing tiny librarian glasses, carefully pinning clear helpful model-care cards onto a garden wall beside a retro terminal; each card has short pixel text like "FAST", "DEEP", and "CREATIVE" while model-name headers are gently crossed out. Lime green (#ecf874) card accents and glowing code-plants bloom among pastel flowers, cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.
